summary: This source is a podcast episode description featuring Fei-Fei Li. It briefly outlines her views on the necessity of spatial intelligence in AI, the shift toward Large World Models (LWMs), and the importance of human-centered AI, human agency, and diversity in technology development.

principles.statement: Spatial intelligence is crucial for the next phase of AI development.
principles.rationale: Giving AI the ability to perceive and interact with the 3D world will lead to major breakthroughs in fields like medicine, climate, and education.

principles.statement: AI development must be human-centered, prioritizing human agency and respect.
principles.rationale: To build a positive future, AI should be used to amplify human potential, requiring proper regulatory guardrails and governance.

principles.statement: Diversity is a critical component of AI development.
principles.rationale: Ensuring diverse voices are included in the creation of AI (such as through programs like AI4ALL) is necessary for building equitable technology.

mental_models.name: Spatial Intelligence / Large World Models (LWMs)
mental_models.description: A paradigm for AI that moves beyond text-based Large Language Models (LLMs) to models capable of perceiving, understanding, and interacting with the 3D physical and digital worlds.
